sections.heading: EXPRESSING ‘PLAN TO’ WITH DỰ ĐỊNH
sections.explanation: “Dự định” means “plan to” and is used when a person or group has a clear plan or idea to accomplish something in the future. It’s similar to “intend to,” but with a slightly stronger sense of having thought about or planned for the action. PATTERN Subject + dự định + verb Tôi dự định đi du lịch vào cuối tuần. I plan to travel this weekend Cô ấy dự định học tiếng Anh She plans to study more English Chúng tôi dự định mở quán cà phê vào tháng tới. We plan to open a café next month. Mẹ tôi dự định tham gia buổi họp ngày mai My mom plans to have a meeting tomorrow Tôi dự định đi du học năm sau I plan to study abroad next year Chồng tôi dự định nghỉ việc tháng sau My husband plans to resign next month

sections.explanation: “Lên kế hoạch” means “make plans to” or “plan to,” and it is often used to emphasize on preparation for projects, events, or detailed personal plans. PATTERN Subject + lên kế hoạch + verb Tôi đang lên kế hoạch đi du lịch vào cuối tuần. I am planning to travel this weekend Cô ấy đang lên kế hoạch học tiếng Anh She is planning to study more English Chúng tôi đang lên kế hoạch mở quán cà phê vào tháng tới. We are planning to open a café next month. faqs.question: How to use Định in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Định” means “to intend to” and is placed before a verb to indicate intention. For example, “Tôi định học tiếng Việt” (I intend to study Vietnamese).

faqs.question: What’s the difference between muốn and định ?
faqs.answer: “Muốn” expresses a desire (want) to do something, while “định” specifically indicates an intention or plan. For example, “Tôi muốn đi du lịch” (I want to travel) versus “Tôi định đi du lịch” (I intend to travel).

faqs.question: Can “sẽ” be used to express intention, or is it only for the future tense?
faqs.answer: “ Sẽ ” generally indicates a future action but can imply intent if the context is clear. However, “ định ” or “muốn” are more specific for intentional actions.

faqs.question: Can “đang định” be used to indicate an immediate intention?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đang định” implies an intention that you are actively considering or about to do soon. For example, “Tôi đang định gọi cho bạn” (I am about to call you).

extracted_patterns: Subject + sẽ + verb

items.title: Expressing surprise in Vietnamese

example.vi: Anh ấy vừa thắng giải lớn thật à?

sections.heading: SURPRISE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing surprise is often vivid and emotional, using short exclamations that convey shock, disbelief, or amazement. This lesson will introduce common phrases like Thật à , Không ngờ , and Trời ơi to help you sound more natural in everyday conversations.

examples.vi: Thật à , Không ngờ , and Trời ơi
examples.en: to help you sound more natural in everyday conversations.

sections.heading: THẬT Á? = REALLY??
sections.explanation: “ Thật à? ” is used to express astonishment upon hearing something unexpected or shocking! It’s similar to “ Really? ” or “ Is that true? ” in English. PATTERN Thật à? + Clause For

examples.vi: ấy vừa thắng giải lớn thật à?
examples.en: He just won a big prize,

examples.vi: really ? Cô ấy có ba đứa con thật à?
really ? Thật à? Bạn sắp đi du học?
study abroad? Bạn đã hoàn thành công việc sớm thế thật à?
examples.en: You finished the work so quickly,

examples.vi: really ? Thật à? Đây là lần đầu tiên bạn đến đây?
examples.en: Really ? This is your first time here?

examples.vi: Anh ta nói trôi chảy tiếng Nhật thật à?

sections.heading: TRỜI ƠI! = OMG!
sections.explanation: “ Trời ơi! ” expresses surprise, shock, or even exasperation. It’s equivalent to “ Oh my God! ” or “ Oh dear! ” in English and is widely used in informal contexts. PATTERN Trời ơi! + Clause For

examples.vi: Trời ơi! Sao anh lại làm vậy?
examples.en: Oh my God! Why did you

sections.heading: KHÔNG NGỜ = TO BE SURPRISED THAT…
sections.explanation: “Không ngờ” expresses that you are surprised at something unexpected. For

examples.vi: Tôi không ngờ anh ấy lại giỏi đến vậy.
examples.en: I didn’t expect him to be

faqs.question: What is the difference between “Thật à?
faqs.answer: ” and “

faqs.question: Thật không?
Both express surprise, but “Thật à?
faqs.answer: ” is more neutral/informal and used in spoken

faqs.question: Vietnamese, while “Thật không?
faqs.answer: ” can carry more disbelief or request for confirmation. Is “Trời ơi!” considered rude or inappropriate? No, “Trời ơi!” is very common and expressive in daily conversation, but tone and context matter. Use it carefully in formal settings.

faqs.question: Can “Không ngờ” be used at the beginning of a sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes , “Không ngờ…” (I didn’t expect…) often starts a sentence to show surprise or shock about an outcome.

faqs.question: Are there any formal ways to express surprise in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: While most surprise expressions are informal, phrases like “Tôi thật sự ngạc nhiên” (I’m truly surprised) are more formal or written.

faqs.question: Can I combine these expressions in one sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes, native speakers often layer expressions for emphasis, like “Trời ơi, không ngờ luôn đó!” , especially in spoken language.

extracted_patterns: Thật à? + Clause For example: Anh ấy vừa thắng giải lớn thật à? He just won a big prize, really ? Cô ấy có ba đứa con thật à? She has three kids, really ?
Trời ơi! + Clause For example: Trời ơi! Sao anh lại làm vậy? Oh my God! Why did you do that? Trời ơi! Món ăn này ngon quá! Oh my God! This dish is so delicious!
Subject + không ngờ + Clause For example: Tôi không ngờ anh ấy lại giỏi đến vậy. I didn’t expect him to be that talented Không ngờ cô ấy đã kết hôn rồi. I’m surprised she’s already married

items.title: Expressing regret in Vietnamese

example.vi: Thật tiếc là tôi không thể tham dự buổi tiệc.

sections.heading: REGRET IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, there are few phrases to express regret or a sense of sorrow, wishing things were different, or acknowledging a missed opportunity. This lesson will show you how to express regret in Vietnamese.

sections.heading: THẬT TIẾC LÀ = IT’S A PITY THAT…
sections.explanation: “Thật tiếc là” means “It’s a pity that” or “It’s unfortunate that.” It’s used to express disappointment about a situation that has happened or is currently happening. PATTERN Thật tiếc là + clause For

examples.vi: Thật tiếc là tôi không thể tham dự buổi tiệc. It’s
examples.en: a pity that I can’t attend

examples.vi: the party Thật tiếc là anh ấy không đồng ý với ý tưởng này. It’s
examples.en: unfortunate that he didn’t agree with

examples.vi: this idea Thật tiếc là chúng ta đã bỏ lỡ cơ hội đó.
examples.en: I t’s a pity that we missed

examples.vi: that opportunity Thật tiếc là thời tiết hôm nay không đẹp. It’s
nice today Thật tiếc là cô ấy đã từ chối lời mời của tôi. It’s
my invitation Thật tiếc là tôi không có đủ thời
examples.en: gian. It’s unfortunate that I don’t have enough time

sections.heading: GIÁ NHƯ = IF ONLY
sections.explanation: “Giá như” expresses deep regret about unrealized scenarios, often referring to the past. PATTERN Giá như + claus e For

examples.vi: Giá như tôi đã chăm chỉ hơn khi còn trẻ.
examples.en: If only I had worked harder when I

examples.vi: was young Giá như cô ấy nhận lời cầu hôn của tôi.
my proposal Giá như chúng ta đến sớm hơn một chút.
examples.en: If only we had arrived a

examples.vi: bit earlier Giá như tôi có thể làm lại từ đầu.
start over Giá như tôi biết trước điều này.
this beforehand Giá như anh ấy không rời đi quá sớm.
examples.en: If only he hadn’t left too soon

sections.heading: ĐÁNG LẼ RA = SHOULD HAVE
sections.explanation: “Đáng lẽ ra” means “should have” or “ought to have,” highlighting regret for a missed responsibility or action. PATTERN Đáng lẽ ra + Clause For

examples.vi: Đáng lẽ ra tôi nên gọi cho anh ấy.
called him Đáng lẽ ra chúng ta không nên đi muộn.
been late Đáng lẽ ra tôi nên học chăm hơn.
studied harder Đáng lẽ ra anh ấy nên nghe theo lời khuyên của bạn.
your advice Đáng lẽ ra cô ấy không nên nói như vậy.
said that Đáng lẽ ra tôi nên cẩn thận hơn.
examples.en: I should have been more careful

sections.heading: BIẾT VẬY THÌ = HAD I KNOWN
sections.explanation: “Biết vậy thì” is used to express regret for a decision or action that could have been avoided For

examples.vi: Biết vậy thì tôi đã không nói điều đó.
examples.en: If I had known, I wouldn’t have

examples.vi: said that Biết vậy thì tôi đã đi sớm hơn.
examples.en: Had I known, I would have

examples.vi: gone earlier Biết vậy thì tôi đã không tin anh ấy.
examples.en: If I had known , I wouldn’t have

examples.vi: trusted him Biết vậy thì tôi đã mang ô
examples.en: theo. Had I known, I would have brought

examples.vi: an umbrella Biết vậy thì tôi đã không tiêu hết tiền.
examples.en: If I had known, I wouldn’t have spent all

examples.vi: the money Biết vậy thì tôi đã nghe lời mẹ.
examples.en: Had I known, I would have listened to my mom

faqs.question: Can I use “đáng lẽ ra” in both spoken and written Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đáng lẽ ra” is used in both formal and informal contexts, but it’s more commonly found in reflective or narrative speech and writing to express regret or missed opportunities.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “thật tiếc là” and “đáng lẽ ra”?
faqs.answer: “Thật tiếc là” simply expresses regret or sympathy over something unfortunate, while “đáng lẽ ra” suggests that something should have happened differently, implying a sense of missed obligation or expectation.

faqs.question: How do I use “biết vậy thì” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Biết vậy thì…” means “If I had known, then…” and is usually followed by a statement indicating what the speaker would have done instead. It expresses regret based on hindsight.

faqs.question: Are there other common phrases to express regret in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, other expressions include giá mà (if only), ước gì (I wish), and lẽ ra (should have), all of which can express similar feelings of regret or missed chances.

faqs.question: What level of Vietnamese do I need to use these structures correctly?
faqs.answer: These expressions are generally suited for learners at the B1 (intermediate) level or above, as they require some familiarity with conditional and past narrative forms.

extracted_patterns: Giá như + claus e For example: Giá như tôi đã chăm chỉ hơn khi còn trẻ. If only I had worked harder when I was young Giá như cô ấy nhận lời cầu hôn của tôi. If only she had accepted my proposal
Đáng lẽ ra + Clause For example: Đáng lẽ ra tôi nên gọi cho anh ấy. I should have called him Đáng lẽ ra chúng ta không nên đi muộn. We shouldn’t have been late
Subject + Biết vậy thì + Clause For example: Biết vậy thì tôi đã không nói điều đó. If I had known, I wouldn’t have said that Biết vậy thì tôi đã đi sớm hơn. Had I known, I would have gone earlier

items.title: Expressing hope in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi hy vọng ngày mai trời sẽ nắng.

sections.heading: HOPE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express hope in Vietnamese using common phrases like hy vọng , mong , and more. These expressions are useful in both casual and formal situations to share your wishes, expectations, or optimism about the future.

examples.vi: you’ll
examples.en: learn how to express hope in Vietnamese using common phrases

examples.vi: like hy vọng
examples.en: , mong , and more. These expressions are useful in both casual and formal situations to share your wishes, expectations, or optimism about the future.

sections.heading: HY VỌNG = HOPE
sections.explanation: “Hy vọng” means “to hope.” It is commonly used in formal and informal settings to express a general wish or expectation for something to happen. For

examples.vi: Tôi hy vọng ngày mai trời sẽ nắng.
sunny tomorrow Chúng tôi hy vọng dự án sẽ thành công.
examples.en: We hope the project will succeed

examples.vi: Play Hy vọng bạn sẽ sớm khỏe lại.
examples.en: I hope you will recover soon.

examples.vi: Play Hy vọng anh ấy đến đúng giờ.
time. Play Tôi hy vọng họ sẽ hiểu ý tôi.
examples.en: I hope they will understand my point.

examples.vi: Play Hy vọng kỳ thi lần này dễ dàng hơn.
examples.en: I hope this exam will be easier

sections.heading: MONG = TO HOPE AND WISH
sections.explanation: “Mong” expresses a more personal wish, often with a tone of politeness. People usually use “mong” to express their needs or requests to someone politely. For

examples.vi: Tôi mong bạn sớm hồi âm.
soon Mong rằng mọi việc sẽ ổn thỏa.
examples.en: I hope that everything will be

examples.vi: fine Anh ấy mong được gặp lại cô ấy.
again Mong bạn hiểu cho tôi.
understand me Chúng tôi mong chuyến đi sẽ thuận lợi.
smoothly Mong mọi người có một ngày vui vẻ.
examples.en: Hope everyone has a happy day

sections.heading: CẦU MONG = TO WISH/ TO PRAY THAT…
sections.explanation: “Cầu mong” is used to express deep hope or prayers, often in situations involving strong emotions, such as worry, gratitude, or love. For

examples.vi: Tôi cầu mong bạn sẽ an toàn.
be safe Chúng tôi cầu mong mọi người bình
stays safe. Cầu mong anh ấy tìm được công việc tốt.
good job Cô ấy cầu mong cha mẹ khỏe mạnh.
health Tôi c ầu mong kỳ thi sẽ diễn ra suôn sẻ.
go smoothly Cầu mong cuộc họp này mang lại kết quả tốt đẹp.
examples.en: I hope this meeting brings good results

sections.heading: ƯỚC = TO WISH
sections.explanation: “ Ước” is often used to express wishes, either realistic or fantastical. It is commonly used in reflective or imaginative contexts. For

examples.vi: Tôi ước mình có thể
could fly Ước gì hôm nay không phải thứ
Monday Tôi ước có nhiều thời gian hơn
time Anh ấy ước được đi du lịch vòng quanh thế giới.
the world Ước gì tôi đã học chăm hơn.
studied harder Cô ấy ước có một ngôi nhà bên bờ biển
examples.en: She wishes for a house by the sea

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “hy vọng” and “mong”?
faqs.answer: “Hy vọng” is a more general and neutral way to express hope. While “mong” can sound slightly more formal or polite and is often used in expressions of wish or expectation.

faqs.question: Can “mong” be used with pronouns like “tôi” or “bạn”?
faqs.answer: Yes , “mong” can be used with personal pronouns. For example: Tôi mong bạn thành công (I hope you succeed).

faqs.question: Is there a grammatical structure I should follow when using “hy vọng”?
faqs.answer: Yes , “hy vọng” is often followed by rằng (that) or a clause, e.g., Tôi hy vọng rằng bạn sẽ đến sớm (I hope that you will come early).

faqs.question: Can “mong” be used in formal writing or letters?
faqs.answer: Absolutely . “Mong” is very common in formal or polite Vietnamese, especially in written requests or closing remarks in emails and letters, like Mong nhận được phản hồi sớm (Hope to receive your reply soon).

faqs.question: At what level should I learn to use “hy vọng” and “mong”?
faqs.answer: These expressions are suitable for A2 to B1 level learners, as they involve basic sentence structures and polite forms commonly used in everyday conversation.

extracted_patterns: Subject + hy vọng + clause For example: Tôi hy vọng ngày mai trời sẽ nắng. I hope it will be sunny tomorrow Chúng tôi hy vọng dự án sẽ thành công. We hope the project will succeed
Subject + mong + clause/ adjective/verb For example: Tôi mong bạn sớm hồi âm. I hope you reply soon Mong rằng mọi việc sẽ ổn thỏa. I hope that everything will be fine
Subject + cầu mong + Clause For example: Tôi cầu mong bạn sẽ an toàn. I pray that you will be safe Chúng tôi cầu mong mọi người bình an. We pray that everyone stays safe.
Subject + ước + Claus e For example: Tôi ước mình có thể bay I wish I could fly Ước gì hôm nay không phải thứ hai I wish today wasn’t Monday

items.title: Probability in Vietnamese

example.vi: Anh ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này.

sections.heading: PROBABILITY IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you will learn how to express probability in Vietnamese using common phrases such as hình như , có lẽ , chắc là , and có thể . These expressions help you talk about uncertainty, possibility, and likelihood in everyday situations.

examples.vi: hình như , có lẽ , chắc là , and có thể
examples.en: . These expressions help you talk about uncertainty, possibility, and likelihood in everyday situations.

sections.heading: CÓ THỂ = MIGHT BE
sections.explanation: “Có thể” is used to express possibility or probability in the present or future. For

examples.vi: ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này.
this problem Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi.
good doctor Dự án này có thể thành công.
might succeed Tôi có thể hoàn thành công việc trước thời hạn.
examples.en: I might finish the work ahead

examples.vi: of schedule Trẻ em có thể học rất

examples.en: nay. They might come this afternoon

sections.heading: CHẮC LÀ = PROBABLY
sections.explanation: “Chắc là” is used to express a slight degree of certainty about an event. PATTERN Chắc là + clause For

examples.vi: Chắc là họ đã về nhà rồi. They’ve
home already Chắc là hôm nay trời sẽ nắng.
sunny today Chắc là cô ấy đang ở văn phòng. She’s
the office Chắc là anh ta đã hiểu sai ý tôi.
my point Chắc là chúng ta sẽ phải đợi thêm. We’ll
wait longer Chắc là anh ấy không muốn
examples.en: tham gia. He probably doesn’t want to participate

sections.heading: HÌNH NHƯ = APPARENTLY
sections.explanation: “Hình như” means “it seems” or “apparently,” indicating speculation based on observations or limited evidence. PATTERN Hình như + Clause For

examples.vi: Hình như anh ấy đang buồn.
feeling sad Hình như họ chưa biết tin này.
examples.en: It seems they don’t know this

examples.vi: news yet Hình như cô ấy không thích món này.
this dish Hình như trời sắp mưa.
to rain Hình như anh ta quên mất cuộc họp.
the meeting Hình như họ chưa quyết định
examples.en: xong. It seems they haven’t finished deciding

sections.heading: CÓ LẼ = MAYBE / PERHAPS
sections.explanation: “Có lẽ” is used to express moderate probability or speculation. For

examples.vi: Có lẽ tôi sẽ đến muộn một chút.
bit late Có lẽ anh ấy không tham dự.
attend Có lẽ trời sẽ đẹp vào ngày
examples.en: mai. Perhaps the weather will be

examples.vi: nice tomorrow Có lẽ cô ấy không nghe rõ.
hear clearly Có lẽ họ đang bận.
busy Có lẽ tôi nên cân nhắc lại quyết định của mình.
examples.en: Maybe I should reconsider my decision

faqs.question: What is the difference between “hình như” and “có lẽ”?
faqs.answer: Both express uncertainty, but hình như often implies personal perception or assumption, while có lẽ is a more neutral way to say “maybe” or “probably.”

faqs.question: Can I use “chắc là” and “có thể” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Not exactly. Chắc là implies higher certainty (“surely”), while có thể suggests possibility without commitment (“might”).

faqs.question: Where do these expressions go in a sentence?
faqs.answer: They typically appear at the beginning or before the verb phrase: e.g., Có lẽ anh ấy chưa đến. (“Maybe he hasn’t arrived.”)

faqs.question: Are these expressions formal or informal?
faqs.answer: They are generally neutral and appropriate in both casual and polite contexts, depending on tone and situation.

faqs.question: How can I practice using these expressions naturally?
faqs.answer: Try making predictions about current events or guessing what might happen using these structures, and get feedback from native speakers or a teacher.

faqs.question: Can I go study in Vietnam with ?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can! You can study with us in Saigon or Hanoi, in both group or individual classes. You’ll even have the opportunity to live with a Vietnamese family, thanks to our homestays .

extracted_patterns: Subject + có thể + Verb For example: Anh ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này. He might solve this problem Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi. She might become a good doctor
Chắc là + clause For example: Chắc là họ đã về nhà rồi. They’ve probably gone home already Chắc là hôm nay trời sẽ nắng. It will probably be sunny today
Hình như + Clause For example: Hình như anh ấy đang buồn. It seems he’s feeling sad Hình như họ chưa biết tin này. It seems they don’t know this news yet
Subject + Có lẽ + Clause For example: Có lẽ tôi sẽ đến muộn một chút. Maybe I’ll be a bit late Có lẽ anh ấy không tham dự. Maybe he won’t attend

items.title: Purpose of action in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi học tiếng Anh để đi du học.

sections.heading: PURPOSE OF ACTION IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express the purpose of an action in Vietnamese using common structures like để , nhằm , and vì . These words help explain why someone does something and are essential for forming clear, goal-oriented sentences.

examples.vi: you’ll
examples.en: learn how to express the purpose of an action in Vietnamese using common

examples.vi: structures like để , nhằm , and vì
examples.en: . These words help explain why someone does something and are essential for forming clear, goal-oriented sentences.

sections.heading: ĐỂ = IN ORDER TO
sections.explanation: “Để” translates to “in order to” or “so that” and is the most common way to express purpose in Vietnamese. For

examples.vi: Tôi học tiếng Anh để đi du học.
study abroad Cô ấy làm việc chăm chỉ để kiếm tiền.
money Anh ấy tập thể dục để giữ sức khỏe.
stay healthy Chúng tôi đến đây để gặp bạn.
meet you Họ mua nhà để ở gần trường học.
examples.en: They bought a house to live near

examples.vi: the school Tôi tiết kiệm tiền để
examples.en: mua xe. I save money to buy a car

sections.heading: VÌ = BECAUSE OF
sections.explanation: “Vì” is used to indicate a reason or motivation for an action. For
sections.pattern: Subject + Verb + vì + Noun/ Purpose Clause

examples.vi: ấy làm việc vì gia đình

sections.heading: NHẰM = WITH THE AIM OF…
sections.explanation: “Nhằm” is used in formal or professional contexts to express intent. For
sections.pattern: Subject + Verb + nhằm + Noun phrase/ Purpose Clause

examples.vi: Công ty tổ chức hội thảo nhằm nâng cao chất lượng dịch vụ
examples.en: The company organizes seminars to improve

sections.heading: CHO = FOR…
sections.explanation: “Cho” is used to express actions done for the benefit or purpose of someone or something. For

her family Chúng tôi tổ chức buổi tiệc cho trẻ

examples.en: I write this letter for my friends

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “để” and “nhằm” when expressing purpose?
faqs.answer: “Để” is commonly used in both spoken and written language to express purpose. “Nhằm” is more formal and often appears in written or official contexts.

faqs.question: Can I use “vì” and “để” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: No. “Vì” introduces the reason or cause of an action, while “để” shows the purpose or intended result. They are not interchangeable.

faqs.question: Where in the sentence should “để” or “nhằm” be placed?
faqs.answer: They are typically placed before a verb or verb phrase to introduce the purpose, e.g., Tôi học tiếng Việt để giao tiếp tốt hơn (I study Vietnamese to communicate better).

faqs.question: Is it okay to omit “để” in casual speech?
faqs.answer: While native speakers might occasionally drop “để” in very casual or rushed speech, it’s generally better for learners to use it clearly to avoid confusion.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “vì muốn” and “để”?
faqs.answer: “Vì muốn” introduces a personal motivation ( because [I] want to… ), while “để” focuses on the intended goal of the action.

extracted_patterns: Subject + Verb + để + Purpose Clause For example: Tôi học tiếng Anh để đi du học. I study English to study abroad Cô ấy làm việc chăm chỉ để kiếm tiền. She works hard to earn money
Subject + Verb + vì + Noun/ Purpose Clause For example: Anh ấy làm việc vì gia đình He works for his family Tôi cố gắng vì tương lai của mình. I strive for my future
Subject + Verb + cho + Someone For example: Tôi mua quà cho bạn. I buy a gift for you Cô ấy làm việc này cho gia đình She does this for her family

items.title: Expressing potential in Vietnamese

example.vi: Anh ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này.

sections.heading: POTENTIAL IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you will learn how to express potential in Vietnamese using phrases like “có thể” , “có cơ hội” , and “có khả năng” . These expressions help you talk about possibilities, opportunities, and abilities in different contexts.

examples.vi: “có thể” , “có cơ hội” , and “có khả năng”
examples.en: . These expressions help you talk about possibilities, opportunities, and abilities in different contexts.

sections.heading: CÓ THỂ = CAN
sections.explanation: “Có thể” is used to express possibility or probability in the present or future. For

examples.vi: ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này.
this problem Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi.
good doctor Dự án này có thể thành công.
can succeed Tôi có thể hoàn thành công việc trước thời hạn.
examples.en: I can finish the work ahead

examples.vi: of schedule Trẻ em có thể học rất
very quickly Họ có thể đến vào chiều
examples.en: nay. They can come this afternoon

sections.heading: CÓ KHẢ NĂNG = TO BE CAPABLE OF
sections.explanation: “Có khả năng” is used to emphasize someone’s or something’s capability or ability. For

examples.vi: ấy có khả năng học rất
examples.en: nhanh. He has the ability to learn

examples.vi: very quickly Dự án này có khả năng thành công
examples.en: cao. This project has a high potential

examples.vi: for success Cô ấy có khả năng thuyết phục người khác.
persuade others Chúng ta có khả năng hoàn thành mục tiêu.
examples.en: We have the potential to achieve

examples.vi: the goal Trẻ em có khả năng thích
examples.en: nghi nhanh. Children have the potential to

examples.vi: adapt quickly Công nghệ này có khả năng thay đổi thế giới.
examples.en: This technology has the potential to change the world

sections.heading: CÓ CƠ HỘI = TO HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY
sections.explanation: “Có cơ hội” is used to express the opportunity of someone to do something. For

examples.vi: Tôi có cơ hội làm việc ở nước ngoài.
work abroad. Cô ấy có cơ hội giành chiến thắng.
to win Họ có cơ hội hợp tác với một công ty lớn.
examples.en: They have the chance to collaborate with a

examples.vi: big company Chúng ta có cơ hội tham gia dự án quốc tế
examples.en: We have the opportunity to join an international

examples.vi: project. Anh ấy có cơ hội được thăng chức.
be promoted Cô ấy không có cơ hội để giải thích.
examples.en: She didn’t have the opportunity to explain

faqs.question: What is the difference between “có thể” and “có khả năng”?
faqs.answer: “Có thể” is often used to express general possibility or permission, while “có khả năng” emphasizes someone’s ability or potential to do something.

faqs.question: Can I use “có cơ hội” in place of “có thể”?
faqs.answer: No, “có cơ hội” refers to having an opportunity, not just the possibility or ability to do something. It’s more context-dependent.

faqs.question: Is “có khả năng” formal or casual?
faqs.answer: “Có khả năng” is more formal and often used in written or professional contexts, while “có thể” is more common in everyday conversation.

faqs.question: How do I negate these expressions of potential?
faqs.answer: You can negate them by adding “không” in front, such as “không thể” (cannot), “không có khả năng” (lack the ability), or “không có cơ hội” (no opportunity).

faqs.question: Can these expressions be used with both verbs and nouns?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially “có thể” and “có khả năng” can be followed by verbs ( có thể đi – can go) or noun clauses ( có khả năng thành công – potential for success).

extracted_patterns: Subject + có thể + Verb For example: Anh ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này. He can solve this problem Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi. She can become a good doctor
Subject + có cơ hội + Verb For example: Tôi có cơ hội làm việc ở nước ngoài. I have the chance to work abroad. Cô ấy có cơ hội giành chiến thắng. She has the opportunity to win

example.vi: Tuy trời mưa nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài

sections.heading: EXPRESS CONTRAST IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing contrast helps you show differences between ideas, actions, or situations. This lesson will teach you how to use structures like “mặc dù” , “trong khi” , and “ngược lại” to clearly communicate opposition or comparison in your sentences.

examples.vi: “mặc dù”
, and “ngược lại”
examples.en: to clearly communicate opposition or comparison in your sentences.

sections.heading: TUY…NHƯNG = ALTHOUGH…
sections.explanation: “ Tuy… nhưng… ” translates to “ Although… ” and is used to express a contrast between two clauses. PATTERN Tuy + clause, nhưng + contrast clause For

examples.vi: trời mưa nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài
examples.en: Although it is raining, we still go out

examples.vi: Tuy anh ấy trẻ, nhưng anh ấy rất thông
examples.en: minh. He is young, but he is very

examples.vi: smart Tuy cô ấy mệt, nhưng cô ấy vẫn tiếp tục làm việc
examples.en: Although she is tired, she continues

examples.vi: working. Tuy bộ phim dài, nhưng nó rất thú vị.
examples.en: The movie is long, but it is

examples.vi: interesting. Tuy thời tiết nóng, nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi leo núi
examples.en: Although the weather is hot, we go hiking

examples.vi: Tuy anh ấy không thích cà phê, nhưng anh ấy uống mỗi sáng
examples.en: He doesn’t like coffee, but he drinks it every morning.

sections.heading: MẶC DÙ…NHƯNG = EVEN THOUGH…
sections.explanation: “ Mặc dù… nhưng… ” is similar to “ Tuy… nhưng… ” and means “ Even though… but… ” It emphasizes the unexpected result or contradiction. PATTERN Mặc dù + clause, nhưng + contrast clause For

examples.vi: Mặc dù trời mưa nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài
examples.en: Even though it is raining, we still

examples.vi: go out Mặc dù anh ấy trẻ, nhưng anh ấy rất thông
examples.en: minh. He is young, but he is

examples.vi: very smart Mặc dù cô ấy mệt, nhưng cô ấy vẫn tiếp tục làm việc
examples.en: Even though she is tired, she

examples.vi: continues working. Mặc dù bộ phim dài, nhưng nó rất thú vị.
examples.en: The movie is long, but it

examples.vi: is interesting. Mặc dù thời tiết nóng, nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi leo núi
examples.en: Even though the weather is hot, we

examples.vi: go hiking Mặc dù anh ấy không thích cà phê, nhưng anh ấy uống mỗi sáng
examples.en: He doesn’t like coffee, but he drinks it every morning.

sections.heading: NGƯỢC LẠI = ON THE CONTRARY
sections.explanation: “ Ngược lại ” means “ on the contrary ” and is used to introduce an opposite idea or result. PATTERN Clause, ngược lại + contrast clause For

examples.vi: ấy thích thể thao, ngược lại
trai anh ấy ghét
examples.en: He loves sports, on the contrary, his brother

examples.vi: hates them. Tôi nghĩ cô ấy buồn, ngược lạ i, cô ấy lại
examples.en: vui I thought she was sad, but on the contrary , she was

examples.vi: happy Anh ấy trượt kỳ thi, ngược lại, bạn anh ấy đậu
examples.en: He failed the test, on the contrary , his friend

examples.vi: passed it. Hôm qua thời tiết lạnh, ngược lại , hôm nay lại ấm
examples.en: The weather was cold yesterday, but today it is warm, on

examples.vi: the contrary. Cô ấy trầm lặng, ngược lại, chị của cô ấy rất hay nói
examples.en: She is quiet, on the contrary, her sister is very

examples.vi: talkative. Anh ấy nhút nhát, nhưng bạn anh ấy, ngược lại , rất hướng ngoại.
examples.en: He is shy, but his friend, on the contrary , is outgoing.

sections.heading: TRONG KHI = WHILE
sections.explanation: “ Trong khi ” means “ while ” and is used to express contrast between two simultaneous actions or states. PATTERN Trong khi + clause, contrast clause For

examples.vi: Tôi đang học, trong khi bạn tôi đang chơi
examples.en: I am studying, while my friend is

examples.vi: playing soccer Cô ấy đang làm việc,
em trai cô ấy đang nghỉ ngơi
examples.en: She is working, while her brother is

examples.vi: resting Anh ấy thích cà phê,
rong khi cô ấy lại thích trà
prefers tea. Tôi đang nấu bữa tối, trong khi chị tôi đang dọn bàn ăn
examples.en: I am cooking dinner, while my sister is setting

examples.vi: the table. Họ đang chờ xe buýt, trong khi chúng tôi đang đi bộ về nhà
examples.en: They are waiting for the bus, while we are walking

examples.vi: home Anh ấy đang ôn
trong khi các bạn của anh ấy đang chơi
examples.en: He is studying for the exam, while his friends are playing games.

faqs.question: Can I use “mặc dù” and “nhưng” in the same sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes . While “mặc dù” introduces the contrast, “nhưng” can be added in the second clause to emphasize it, similar to “although… but…” in English. For example: Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi học.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “trong khi” and “ngược lại”?
faqs.answer: “Trong khi” means “while” and is used to contrast two simultaneous actions or situations. “Ngược lại” means “on the contrary” or “in contrast” and introduces an opposite idea or result.

faqs.question: Is “mặc dù” formal or informal?
faqs.answer: “Mặc dù” is neutral and commonly used in both spoken and written Vietnamese. It is slightly more formal than “dù” but both are acceptable in most contexts.

faqs.question: Can I start a sentence with “ngược lại”?
faqs.answer: Yes . “Ngược lại” can begin a sentence to introduce a contrasting idea. Example: Tôi thích ở nhà. Ngược lại, anh ấy thích đi chơi.

faqs.question: Are there other ways to express contrast in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes. Besides “mặc dù”, “trong khi”, and “ngược lại”, you can also use “tuy nhiên” (however), “dù sao đi nữa” (anyway), and “trái lại” (conversely) depending on the tone and context.

items.title: Giving advice in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi nghĩ bạn nên quên anh ấy đi vì anh ấy không yêu bạn đâu!

sections.heading: GIVING ADVICE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Giving advice is a common part of everyday conversation in Vietnamese. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use expressions like khuyên , thử , and nghĩ to offer suggestions, make recommendations, and gently guide others in a natural and culturally appropriate way.

examples.vi: you’ll
expressions like khuyên , thử , and nghĩ
examples.en: to offer suggestions, make recommendations, and gently guide others in a natural and culturally appropriate way.

sections.heading: TÔI NGHĨ BẠN NÊN… = I THINK YOU SHOULD…
sections.explanation: This is one of the most direct ways to offer advice in Vietnamese. It translates to “I think you should…” and is followed by the verb in its root form . This phrase is polite and widely used. For

examples.vi: Tôi nghĩ bạn nên quên anh ấy đi vì anh ấy không yêu bạn đâu!
examples.en: I think you should forget him because he doesn’t

examples.vi: love you! Tôi nghĩ bạn nên cố gắng hơn
examples.en: I think you should try harder.

examples.vi: View More Tôi nghĩ anh ấy nên nói xin lỗi và giải thích tất cả cho bạn
examples.en: I think he should apologize and explain everything to

examples.vi: you. Anh ấy nghĩ bạn nên xem anh ấy chỉ là kỷ niệm đẹp
examples.en: He thinks you should see him as just a

examples.vi: beautiful memory. Mẹ nghĩ tôi nên học đại học trước, sau đó đi làm
examples.en: My mom thinks I should go to college first, then

examples.vi: work. Ba nghĩ mẹ nên bình tĩnh trước khi nói
examples.en: My dad thinks my mom should calm down before speaking.

sections.heading: BẠN THỬ…XEM SAO? = YOU SHOULD TRY…?
sections.explanation: This phrase suggests that the other person should give something a try . It is more casual and often used when offering suggestions or alternatives. For

examples.vi: Bạn cô đơn. Bạn thử yêu
examples.en: xem sao? You are lonely. You should try loving

examples.vi: someone. Anh chưa viết CV bao giờ. Anh thử viết
examples.en: CV xem sao? He has never written a CV. He should try writing

examples.vi: one. Em khó ngủ. Em thử ăn táo trước khi ngủ
examples.en: xem sao? You have trouble sleeping. You should try eating an apple before

examples.vi: bed. Em mệt mỏi. Em thử đi spa thư giãn
examples.en: xem sao? You’re tired. You should try going to a spa

examples.vi: to relax. Bạn nhớ cô ấy. Bạn thử nhắn tin cho cô ấy
examples.en: xem sao? You miss her. You should try

examples.vi: texting her. Bạn mông lung. Bạn thử viết nhật ký
examples.en: xem sao? You’re confused. You should try writing a journal.

sections.heading: KHUYÊN… = TO RECOMMEND / TO ADVISE
sections.explanation: This phrase is a more formal way to give advice. It is suitable for serious or professional contexts For

examples.vi: Chị khuyên em đi học đi!
examples.en: I advise you to go to

examples.vi: school! Anh khuyên em nên có ước mơ của riêng mình!
examples.en: I advise you to have your

examples.vi: own dreams! Họ khuyên tôi nên cưới anh ấy
him. Ba mẹ khuyên tôi không nên yêu anh ấy
examples.en: My parents advise me not to

examples.vi: love him. Tôi khuyên bạn nên từ bỏ vì chuyện tình yêu này không có tương
examples.en: lai I advise you to give up because this relationship has

examples.vi: no future. Tôi khuyên bạn nên lựa chọn chúng tôi thay vì họ
examples.en: I advise you to choose us instead of them.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “khuyên” and “góp ý”?
faqs.answer: Khuyên is used for giving advice with the intent to help or guide someone, often from a position of more experience. Góp ý is more about offering suggestions or constructive feedback, often in a neutral or collaborative tone.

faqs.question: Can I use “nên” instead of “khuyên” when giving advice?
faqs.answer: Yes. Nên is often used directly in sentences to suggest what someone should do (e.g. Bạn nên nghỉ ngơi. ). Khuyên typically introduces the act of advising (e.g. Tôi khuyên bạn nên nghỉ ngơi. ).

faqs.question: Is “thử” always used to suggest something?
faqs.answer: Not always. Thử literally means “to try.” It can be used to suggest trying something ( Bạn thử làm cái này đi. ), but can also describe an attempt ( Tôi thử học tiếng Việt. ).

faqs.question: How do I soften my advice to make it sound more polite?
faqs.answer: You can add softeners like nhé , xem sao , or use a questioning tone (e.g.

faqs.question: Bạn thử làm như vậy xem sao nhé?
faqs.answer: ) to avoid sounding too direct.

faqs.question: When should I use “nghĩ là” in giving advice?
faqs.answer: Nghĩ là (I think that…) introduces a personal opinion and is often used to gently suggest an action, especially when you don’t want to impose (e.g. Tôi nghĩ là bạn nên nói chuyện với cô ấy. ).

extracted_patterns: Subject + khuyên + clause For example: Chị khuyên em đi học đi! I advise you to go to school! Anh khuyên em nên có ước mơ của riêng mình! I advise you to have your own dreams!

items.title: How to order food in Vietnamese
items.pattern: Cho + 1st pronoun + counter + name of the dish or drink

example.vi: Cho tôi 1 tô phở bò

sections.heading: HOW TO ORDER IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: When dining in Vietnam, knowing how to order food and ask for the bill is essential for a smooth experience. In this lesson, we’ll go over the most common phrases and structures used when ordering at a restaurant in Vietnam.

examples.vi: we’ll
examples.en: go over the most common phrases and structures used when ordering at a restaurant in Vietnam.

sections.heading: CHO TÔI… = GIVE ME…
sections.explanation: To order something, we often start with “Cho tôi” , which means “Give me” . After that, you can mention the number , counter (like a unit for measuring items), and the name of the dish or drink . 1st pronoun (tôi, em, con, anh, chị, etc) + counter + name of the dish or drink NOTE || To master ordering food in Vietnamese, you should review the lessons of Numbers in Vietnamese & Counters in Vietnamese Here is a list of 10 popular dishes/drinks in Vietnamese: Phở bò→ Cho tôi 1 tô phở bò Beef noodle→ Give me 1 bowl of beef noodle Bánh mì→ Cho em 2 ổ bánh mì Banh mi (Vietnamese bread) → Give me 2 Vietnamese breads Cà phê sữa→ Cho anh 1 ly cà phê sữa Milk Coffee → Give me 1 glass of milk coffee Nước mắm→ Cho tôi 1 chén nước mắm Fish sauce → Give me 1 small plate of fish sauce Trà đá→ Cho tôi 3 ly trà đá Iced Tea → Give me 3 glasses of iced tea Bánh cuốn→ Cho tôi 1 phần bánh cuốn Banh cuon → GIve me 1 portion of banh cuon Cơm gà→ Cho tôi 5 dĩa cơm gà Chicken rice→ GIve me 5 plates of chicken rice Nước→ Cho anh 3 chai nước Water→ GIve me 3 bottles of water Bia→ Cho anh 5 ly bia Beer→ Give me 5 glasses of beer Sinh tố→ Cho anh 4 ly sinh tố Smoothie→ Give me 4 glasses of smoothie Rượu→ Cho anh 5 chai rượu Wine→ Give me 5 bottles of wine

sections.heading: ASKING ABOUT PRICE OR PAYMENT
sections.explanation: You might want to know how much your meal costs or ask for the bill. Here are some helpful phrases: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? How much is it? Tính tiền! Bill, please Tôi trả bằng tiền mặt I pay in cash Tôi trả bằng chuyển khoản / thẻ I pay by bank account / card

sections.heading: ASKING ABOUT WAIT TIME
sections.explanation: Sometimes, you might want to know how long it will take for your food to arrive or how long you have to wait for a table. These questions are quite common at restaurants. Phải đợi bao nhiêu phút? Phải đợi bao lâu? How many minutes do I have to wait?How long do I have to wait? Khi nào có món? When will the dish be ready? Có bàn không? Is there an available table?

sections.heading: DINING IN OR TAKE-AWAY
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese restaurants, you’ll be asked whether you’re eating here or taking your food to go . Here are some common ways to express that: Ăn ở đây Eat here Mang về Take-away

sections.heading: EXAMPLES
sections.explanation: View Dialogue 1 Customer: Chào anh! Cho tôi một bát phở bò và một ly cà phê sữa đá. (Hello! Give me one bowl of beef pho and one iced coffee with condensed milk.) Waiter: Dạ, anh muốn ăn ở đây hay mang về? (Yes, would you like to eat here or take it to go?) Customer: Ăn ở đây. (I’ll eat here.) Waiter: Vâng, anh phải đợi khoảng 10 phút. (Okay, you’ll have to wait about 10 minutes.) Customer: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? (How much is this?) Waiter: Tổng cộng là 80.000 đồng. (The total is 80,000 VND.) Customer: Tính tiền cho tôi nhé. (Bill, please.) Waiter: Dạ, cảm ơn anh. (Okay, thank you.) View Dialogue 2 Customer: Chào cô! Cho tôi một suất cơm gà và một chai nước suối. (Hello! Give me one serving of chicken rice and one bottle of mineral water.) Vendor: Anh muốn ăn ở đây hay mang về? (Do you want to eat here or take it to go?) Customer: Mang về. (Take it to go.) Vendor: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? (How much is this?) Customer: Tính tiền luôn nhé! (Please give me the bill!) Vendor: Dạ, anh phải đợi khoảng 5 phút nữa. Customer: Tôi sẽ đợi. Cảm ơn! (I’ll wait. Thank you!)

faqs.question: What is the most polite way to order food in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The most polite way is to use “Cho tôi…” (Give me…) or “Tôi muốn…” (I would like…). You can also add “làm ơn” (please) to sound more courteous.

faqs.question: You can say: “Cho tôi xem thực đơn được không?
faqs.answer: ” which means “

faqs.question: May I see the menu?
How do I ask for the bill at a restaurant?
faqs.answer: To ask for the bill, simply say: “Tính tiền, nhé!” or more politely, “Làm ơn cho tôi xin hóa đơn.”

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “món này” and “cái này” when ordering?
faqs.answer: “Món này” refers to this dish (used specifically for food), while “cái này” means this thing in general and is less specific when talking about food.

faqs.question: Is it okay to point at the menu when ordering?
faqs.answer: Yes, it’s quite common and acceptable to politely point at the menu while saying the name of the dish or “Tôi muốn món này.”

extracted_patterns: Cho + 1st pronoun (tôi, em, con, anh, chị, etc) + counter + name of the dish or drink

sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use “đến mức” in Vietnamese to express the extent or result of an action or quality — similar to “so… that” or “to the point that” in English. This structure helps add depth and clarity when describing intensity or consequences.

faqs.question: What does “đến mức” mean in Vietnamese grammar?
faqs.answer: “Đến mức” means “to the extent that” or “so…that” and is used to express the degree or result of an action or description.

faqs.question: How is “đến mức” different from “quá… đến nỗi”?
faqs.answer: While both indicate result or extent, “quá… đến nỗi” emphasizes excessiveness, whereas “đến mức” can describe both moderate and extreme degrees more neutrally.

faqs.question: Can I use “đến mức” in both spoken and written Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đến mức” is appropriate in both contexts and is commonly used in formal and informal situations to show consequence or intensity.

faqs.question: Where does “đến mức” appear in the sentence?
faqs.answer: “Đến mức” typically follows an adjective or verb phrase and precedes a clause or descriptive phrase showing the outcome or degree.

faqs.question: Are there synonyms for “đến mức” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes , phrases like “tới mức” or “đến nỗi” can be used similarly, though they may differ slightly in tone or emphasis depending on context.

extracted_patterns: Subject + adjective + đến mức + clause
Subject + adjective + đến nỗi + clause

items.title: Nominalization in Vietnamese

example.vi: Sự thành công

sections.heading: NOMINALIZATION IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Nominalization (danh từ hóa) helps transform actions and qualities into abstract nouns , making your sentences sound more natural and academic. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use nominalization correctly and effectively in Vietnamese! There are two main ways to create nominalization in Vietnamese:

examples.vi: từ hóa)
examples.en: helps transform actions and qualities into abstract nouns , making your sentences sound more natural and academic. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use nominalization correctly and effectively in Vietnamese! There are two main ways to create nominalization in Vietnamese:

sections.heading: SỰ
sections.explanation: The word “sự” is often used to nominalize adjectives and some verbs , making them function as nouns. PATTERN Sự + Verb/Adjective → Noun For

examples.vi: Thất bại (v) → Sự thất bại
Failure (n) Thành công (v) → Sự thành công
(n) Thay đổi (v) → Sự thay đổi
(n) Xinh đẹp (adj) → Sự xinh đẹp
Beauty (n) Xấu xí (adj) → Sự xấu xí
Ugliness (n) Sạch sẽ (adj) → Sự sạch sẽ

sections.heading: VIỆC
sections.explanation: The word “việc” is used to nominalize specific tasks, work, or responsibilities. PATTERN Việc + Verb→ Noun (Task/Responsibility) For

examples.vi: Học (v) → Việc học
Studying (n) Làm (v) → Việc làm
(n) Pha trà (v) → Việc pha trà

(n) Pha trà (v) → Chuyện pha trà

(v) → Chuyện

examples.vi: TV (n) Viết sách (v) → Chuyện viết sách
examples.en: (n) To write a book (v) → Book writing (n)

sections.note: You can use “Chuyện” as an alternative to “Việc” when you want to refer to a “matter”, “thing”, “story” in an informal and friendly way.

sections.heading: SENTENCE EXAMPLES
sections.explanation: Chuyện học của em như thế nào? How is your studying going? Chuyện làm freelancer của em tới đâu rồi? How is your freelancing going? Chuyện pha trà không khó nhưng cần sự nhẫn nại Making tea is not difficult, but it requires patience. Việc nói chuyện bị cấm trong thư viện Talking is prohibited in the library. Việc xem TV nhiều sẽ hại đến mắt Watching TV too much will harm your eyes. Việc viết sách giúp bạn thông minh hơn Writing books helps you become smarter. Sự thất bại cho tôi nhiều bài học trong cuộc sống Failure has taught me many lessons in life. Sự thành công luôn cần sự kiên trì Success always requires persistence. Em mong anh sẽ có sự thay đổi trong tương lai I hope you will have a change in the future. Cô ấy có một sự xinh đẹp truyền thống She has a traditional beauty . Anh ấy có một sự xấu xí trên ngoại hình nhưng tâm hồn anh ấy đẹp He has an unattractive appearance , but his soul is beautiful. Em cần sự sạch sẽ trong căn phòng này I need cleanliness in this room.

faqs.question: What is nominalization in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Nominalization is the process of turning verbs or adjectives into nouns using words like sự , việc , or chuyện , allowing abstract ideas or actions to be expressed as nouns.

faqs.question: When should I use “sự” instead of “việc” or “chuyện”?
faqs.answer: Use sự for formal, abstract nouns (e.g. sự thành công – success), while việc refers more to actions or tasks ( việc học – the act of studying), and chuyện is used for events or stories, often informal.

faqs.question: Can I use “sự” before any verb?
faqs.answer: Not all verbs can be nominalized with sự . It typically applies to Sino-Vietnamese words and more formal verbs. Native speakers may not use it with purely native or colloquial words.

faqs.question: Is there a difference between “việc học” and “sự học”?
faqs.answer: Yes. Việc học emphasizes the activity of studying, while sự học is more abstract and formal, referring to the concept or process of education in a broader sense.

faqs.question: Is nominalization common in spoken Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Việc and chuyện are quite common in everyday speech, while sự appears more frequently in written or formal contexts like news, reports, or academic texts.

extracted_patterns: Sự + Verb/Adjective → Noun For example: Thất bại (v) → Sự thất bại (n) To fail (v) → Failure (n) Thành công (v) → Sự thành công (n) To succeed (v) → Success (n)
Việc + Verb→ Noun (Task/Responsibility) For example: Học (v) → Việc học (n) To study (v) → Studying (n) Làm (v) → Việc làm (n) To work (v) → Task / Job (n)

items.title: Passive voice in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi được dắt đi bơi bởi ba tôi

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE PASSIVE VOICE
sections.explanation: Vietnamese speakers usually use a positive passive voice when they want to stay humble and show their respect to the other person or to express their honor by doing something. We guide you through the structure and usages of được and bị , helping you express actions from a recipient’s perspective.

examples.vi: được and bị
examples.en: , helping you express actions from a recipient’s perspective.

sections.heading: AFFIRMATIVE
sections.explanation: When you want to express the action being done by someone/something in a positive way, please follow either 1 or 2 patterns below: PATTERN #1 Subject + được + Verb + bởi + someone/something
sections.pattern: #2 Subject + được + someone/something + Verb For example:

examples.vi: dắt tôi đi bơi ôi được dắt đi bơi bởi ba tôi Tôi được ba dắt đi bơi
examples.en: Dad takes me to go swimming (1) I am taken to go swimming by dad (2) I am ( by dad) take to

examples.vi: go swimming Mẹ dạy tôi nấu ăn Tôi được dạy nấu ăn bởi mẹ tôi Tôi được mẹ dạy nấu ăn
examples.en: Mom teaches me how to cook (1) I am taught how to cook by mom (2) I am ( by mom) taught how to cook Active Passive

examples.vi: Active Passive Tôi làm bánh cho bạn trai tôi ăn Bạn trai ăn bánh được làm bởi tôi Bạn trai được tôi làm bánh cho ăn
examples.en: I bake a cake for my boyfriend to eat (1 )My boyfriend eats a cake baked by me (2) My boyfriend has me baked a cake for him

examples.vi: to eat Tôi đọc sách cho em gái nghe Em gái nghe sách được đọc bởi tôi Em gái được tôi đọc sách
examples.en: cho nghe I read book for my sister to listen (1) My sister listens to a book read by me (2) My sister has me read book for her to listen

sections.heading: NEGATIVE
sections.explanation: You just need to add “không” (means “not”) before “được” to make a negative sentence: PATTERN #1 Subject + không được + Verb + bởi + someone/something
sections.pattern: #2 Subject + không được + someone/something + Verb Examples:

examples.vi: không dắt tôi đi bơi Tôi không được dắt đi bơi bởi ba tôi Tôi không được ba dắt đi bơi
examples.en: Dad does not take me to go swimming (1) I am not taken to go swimming by dad (2) I am ( by dad) not taken to

examples.en: Mom does not teach me how to cook (1) I am not taught how to cook by mom (2) I am ( by mom) not taught how to cook Active Passive

faqs.question: How to form the passive voicce in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The passive voice is typically formed using “bị” (when the action has a negative outcome) or “được” (when the action has a positive or neutral outcome) before the main verb. For example, “Tôi bị mắng” (I was scolded) and “Tôi được khen” (I was praised).

faqs.question: Should I use “bị” or “được” in the passive voice?
faqs.answer: Use “bị” when the action has an undesirable effect on the subject (e.g., “Tôi bị mất ví” – “I had my wallet stolen”). Use “được” when the action benefits the subject or is neutral (e.g., “Tôi được tăng lương” – “I got a raise”).

faqs.question: Is the passive voice commonly used?
faqs.answer: The passive voice is less common in Vietnamese compared to English. Active voice is preferred unless it’s necessary to focus on the subject receiving the action.

faqs.question: Can passive be used with all verbs?
faqs.answer: No, the passive voice is generally used with transitive verbs (verbs that can take a direct object). Not all verbs in Vietnamese can form a passive sentence.

items.title: Quoting someone in Vietnamese

example.vi: Anh ấy nói rằng: “Anh yêu em"

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE QUOTES
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, quoting can be done either directly or indirectly, similar to how it is in English. This lesson will explain how to form both direct and indirect quotes, highlighting the differences in structure and word choice.

sections.heading: DIRECT QUOTING
sections.explanation: Direct quoting is the practice of reproducing someone’s exact words or statements, typically enclosed in quotation marks. In writing, this allows you to present another person’s speech, writing, or thoughts verbatim, without altering their meaning or form. For
sections.pattern: Subject + quoting words + rằng (that) + “speech” There are few common quoting words you can memorize right now in

examples.vi: “I
examples.en: will meet you at 5 PM.” This preserves the original wording and can be used for accuracy, emphasis, or clarity in communication. If you want to quote someone directly, just follow this

: “…” Hát → Hát rằng: “…”

sections.heading: INDIRECT QUOTING
sections.explanation: Indirect quoting, also known as indirect speech or reported speech, is when you paraphrase someone’s words rather than quoting them exactly. The original statement is rephrased, and quotation marks are not used. Indirect quotes often involve changes in tense or pronouns to fit the context. For
sections.pattern: Subject + quoting words + rằng (that) + speech clause

examples.vi: “I
examples.en: will meet you at 5 PM.” Indirect quote: He said that he would meet me at 5 PM. In indirect quoting, you’re conveying the same message without using the speaker’s exact words. If you want to quote someone indirectly, just follow this pattern: NOTE || When you report speech of someone, you change the personal pronouns from direct speech into indirect

faqs.question: How do you form direct quotes in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Direct quotes are enclosed in quotation marks, and the speaker’s exact words are used without changes. For example: “Anh ấy nói, ‘Tôi sẽ đi làm sớm.'”

faqs.question: What difference between direct and indirect?
faqs.answer: Direct quoting uses the speaker’s exact words, while indirect quoting paraphrases the statement, often involving changes in tense, pronouns, or sentence structure. For example: “Anh ấy nói rằng anh ấy sẽ đi làm sớm.”

faqs.question: Do quotation marks get used in indirect quotes in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: No, quotation marks are only used in direct quotes. Indirect quotes do not require quotation marks and often start with a reporting verb like “nói rằng” (said that).

faqs.question: Can indirect quotes omit the word “rằng” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, in informal speech, “rằng” (that) can sometimes be omitted in indirect quotes. For example, “Anh ấy nói anh ấy sẽ đến” (He said he would come) can omit “rằng.”

items.title: Expressing hearsay in Vietnamese

example.vi: Nghe nói là công ty sẽ đóng cửa.

sections.heading: EXPRESSING HEARSAY IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing hearsay or indirect information is essential for everyday communication, especially when you’re not the original source of the news. This lesson introduces common structures like nói rằng , nghe nói là , đồn rằng , and theo , helping you share what you’ve heard or read from others.

examples.vi: structures like nói rằng , nghe nói là , đồn rằng
examples.en: , and theo , helping you share what you’ve heard or read from others.

sections.heading: NGHE NÓI LÀ… = I HEARD THAT…

sections.explanation: This phrase highlights that the information comes from another person. For

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “nghe nói là” and “đồn rằng”?
faqs.answer: “Nghe nói là” is often used when the speaker has personally heard something from someone, while “đồn rằng” implies that something is a rumor or gossip that is being spread around.

faqs.question: Can I use “nói rằng” in both written and spoken Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes , “nói rằng” is neutral and can be used in both contexts, though in casual conversation, people often omit “rằng” and use just “nói…” .

faqs.question: Is “theo” always followed by a source?
faqs.answer: Yes, “theo” is used to introduce the source of the information (e.g., “Theo báo Tuổi Trẻ…” ), similar to “according to” in English.

faqs.question: Are these hearsay expressions interchangeable?
faqs.answer: Not always. While they all relate to reporting information, they differ in tone and context. For example, “nghe nói” is more casual, while “nói rằng” is more formal or direct.

faqs.question: Can these structures be used to report opinions as well as facts?
faqs.answer: Yes , hearsay expressions in Vietnamese can be used to report both factual information and personal opinions, depending on the context.

items.title: Expressing degree in Vietnamese

example.vi: Món canh hơi mặn

sections.heading: EXPRESS DEGREE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express different degrees or intensities in Vietnamese using words like hết sức , rất , càng ngày càng , and quá . These expressions help you emphasize how strong, extreme, or gradual an action or quality is, allowing for more vivid and accurate communication.

examples.vi: you’ll
examples.en: learn how to express different degrees or intensities in Vietnamese using

examples.vi: words like hết sức , rất , càng ngày càng , and quá
examples.en: . These expressions help you emphasize how strong, extreme, or gradual an action or quality is, allowing for more vivid and accurate communication.

sections.heading: HƠI / MỘT CHÚT… = LITTLE
sections.explanation: “H ơi / Một chút ” means “ a little ” or “ slightly ” and is used to indicate a mild or slight degree. PATTERN Hơi + Adjective Adjective + Một chút For

sections.heading: CÀNG NGÀY CÀNG… = MORE AND MORE
sections.explanation: “ Càng ngày càng ” means “ more and more ” and is used to express a progressive increase in degree or intensity over time. PATTERN Càng ngày càng + Adjective For

sections.heading: QUÁ… = TOO
sections.explanation: The structure “ quá ” is used to indicate that something is excessively or extremely high in degree. It is equivalent to “ too ” or “excessively” in English. PATTERN Quá + Adjective For

examples.en: The weather is too hot today

sections.heading: RẤT = VERY
sections.explanation: “ Rất ” is used to indicate a high degree of a quality or action, similar to “ very ” or “ extremely ” in English. While not always excessive, it often emphasizes intensity. PATTERN Rất + Adjective For

for me Món ăn rất
very spicy Bây giờ đã rất muộn để ra ngoài It’s

continue working Bộ phim rất dài.
very long Thời tiết hôm nay rất nóng
examples.en: The weather is very hot today

sections.heading: HẾT SỨC = TRY ONESELF BEST
sections.explanation: This phrase emphasizes maximum effort or degree in performing an action. PATTERN Adjective / Verb + hết sức Hết sức + Adjective/verb For

examples.vi: Cô ấy làm việc hết sức để hoàn thành dự án
examples.en: She worked very hard to finish

examples.en: I will try my best to help

examples.vi: you. Anh ấy đang học tiếng Anh hết sức
examples.en: He is doing his best to

the utmost Họ đang cố gắng hết sức để giành chiến thắng trong cuộc
examples.en: thi They are trying their best to win the competition

faqs.question: What is the difference between “rất” and “hết sức”?
faqs.answer: Both mean “very,” but “hết sức” is more intense and formal than “rất” . “Hết sức” can translate to “ extremely ” or “utmost.”

faqs.question: Can I use “quá” in both positive and negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes , but “quá” often carries a negative tone (e.g., nóng quá! = “too hot!”), unless it’s clearly meant to emphasize excitement or praise.

faqs.question: How do I use “càng ngày càng”?
faqs.answer: “Càng ngày càng” means “more and more” or “increasingly.” It is used before an adjective or verb to show a growing degree over time (e.g., càng ngày càng đẹp = “more and more beautiful”).

faqs.question: Is it okay to combine these words in one sentence?
faqs.answer: Usually, no. Since each word already emphasizes degree, combining them (e.g., rất quá đẹp ) would sound unnatural. Use only one degree modifier per idea. Which of these words is used in formal vs. informal contexts? “Rất” and “quá” are used in both casual and formal situations. “Hết sức” is more formal, while “càng ngày càng” is neutral and suits all contexts.

Càng ngày càng + Adjective For example: Thời tiết càng ngày càng lạnh The weather is getting colder and colder . Thành phố càng ngày càng đông đúc. The city is becoming more and more crowded

Rất + Adjective For example: Cái áo này rất lớn đối với tôi. This shirt is very big for me Món ăn rất cay The food is very spicy

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY ONLY IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson we will learn how to use chỉ and mỗi to express “only” in Vietnamese. Both are used to convey exclusivity or limitations, and need to be used correctly in a sentence. Let’s have a look together.

sections.pattern: Subject + chỉ + verb/adjective Em chỉ thích anh! I only love you! Em chỉ giỏi môn Toán I am good at Maths only Em chỉ ngủ khi trời sáng I only sleep in the morning

faqs.question: How to say only in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Only in Vietnamese can be expressed using the words chỉ and mỗi , as well as both words under the expression chỉ…mỗi… .

faqs.question: Love you only How to use “chỉ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: This is a general term used to indicate that something is limited or restricted. Subject + chỉ + verb/adjective Em chỉ ngủ khi trời sáng. I only sleep in the morning.

faqs.question: How to say if in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như. Nếu/ Nếu như + Clause 1, Clause 2 Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá! If you can’t come, let me know! — Learn more about how to say if in Vietnamese here.

PATTERN Thay vì + clause A, clause B (alternative option) Thay vì chị chia tay anh ấy, chị nên lắng nghe anh ấy giải thích Instead of breaking up with him, you should listen to his explanation Thay vì ăn bánh mì, mình muốn ăn phở Instead of eating banh mi, I want to eat Pho Thay vì đi du lịch Nhật Bản, mình muốn đi du lịch Hàn Quốc Instead of traveling to Japan, I want to travel to Korea View More

faqs.question: How to say instead in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express doing one thing “instead” of doing another thing in Vietnamese is Thay vào đó . It is used to introduce an alternative or replacement action, often after stating what was done or considered. It’s like saying “in place of that” or “instead of that.” Clause A + Thay vào đó + clause B (alternative option) Anh ngủ ở nhà cả ngày rồi! Thay vào đó, anh nên chạy bộ 5 phút You’ve been sleeping all day!You should go running in 5 minutes instead !

faqs.question: How to say instead of in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express doing one thing “instead” of doing another in Vietnamese, the following structure is used. It introduces an alternative action or choice that replaces something else.

faqs.question: How to say if not in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: If not in Vietnamese is Nếu không . You need to use the following structure: Nếu không + Verb, Clause Nếu không thích, anh cứ về If (you) not like it, just leave — Learn more about if in Vietnamese here.

extracted_patterns: Thay vì + clause A, clause B (alternative option) Thay vì chị chia tay anh ấy, chị nên lắng nghe anh ấy giải thích Instead of breaking up with him, you should listen to his explanation

items.title: How to say "even though" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài

sections.heading: EVEN THOUGH IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: The phrase “even though” is commonly expressed as a contrast between two clauses. The primary patterns used in Vietnamese for “even though” are “mặc dù,” “dù,” and “tuy…nhưng.” All these expressions indicate a contrast or concession.

examples.vi: “even though”
examples.en: is commonly expressed as a contrast between two clauses. The primary patterns used in Vietnamese for “even though”

examples.vi: are “mặc dù,” “dù,” and “tuy…nhưng.”
examples.en: All these expressions indicate a contrast or concession.

sections.heading: MẶC DÙ … NHƯNG
sections.explanation: “Mặc dù…nhưng” emphasizes the contrast between two clauses, similar to “even though” or “despite” in English. It’s commonly used in both spoken and written language, especially in formal or official contexts. 1, nhưng + Clause 2 Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài Even though it rains, I still go outside Mặc dù cô ấy bận, nhưng cô vẫn giúp tôi Even though she is busy, she still helps me Mặc dù họ không thích món ăn này, nhưng họ vẫn ăn Even though they don’t like this dish, they still eat it Mặc dù anh ấy không khỏe, nhưng anh đi làm Even though he is not well, he goes to work Mặc dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer Mặc dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng Even though the task is difficult, I still try

sections.heading: DÙ … NHƯNG
sections.explanation: “Mặc dù…nhưng” emphasizes the contrast between two clauses, similar to “even though” or “despite” in English. It’s commonly used in both spoken and written language, especially in formal or official contexts. 1, nhưng + Clause 2 Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài Even though it rains, I still go outside Mặc dù cô ấy bận, nhưng cô vẫn giúp tôi Even though she is busy, she still helps me Mặc dù họ không thích món ăn này, nhưng họ vẫn ăn Even though they don’t like this dish, they still eat it Mặc dù anh ấy không khỏe, nhưng anh đi làm Even though he is not well, he goes to work Mặc dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer Mặc dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng Even though the task is difficult, I still try DÙ … NHƯNG “Dù…nhưng” is often used interchangeably with “mặc dù” but is less formal. It conveys a similar idea of contrast and can be used more in a daily conversation. PATTERN Dù + Clause 1, nhưng + Clause 2 Dù trời mưa, nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi dạo Even though it rains, we still go for a walk Dù không ngủ đủ, nhưng tôi vẫn cảm thấy tỉnh táo Even though I didn’t sleep enough, I still feel awake Dù có căng thẳng, nhưng tôi vẫn giữ bình tĩnh Even though I’m stressed, I still remain calm Dù là cuộc thi khó, nhưng tôi vẫn tham gia Even though it’s a difficult competition, I still participate Dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer Dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng Even though the task is difficult, I still try

Even though the road is jammed, they still arrive on time Tuy không thích ăn rau, nhưng tôi vẫn ăn để khỏe Even though i don’t like eating vegetables, I still eat them to be healthy Tuy việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng Even though I have many tasks, I still make time for family

faqs.question: How to say “even though” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, “even though” can be expressed with phrases like mặc dù, dù, or tuy.

faqs.question: Can I use “mặc dù” and “dù” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Yes, mặc dù and dù both mean “even though,” but mặc dù is more formal, while dù is often used in informal contexts.

faqs.question: How to say “with” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom — Learn more about how to use with in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: Check out our Vietnamese Grammar Bank , where you will find free lessons for levels A1 to B1. If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

items.title: How to say "for now" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tạm thời, tôi sẽ ở lại đây

sections.heading: FOR NOW IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, the phrase “tạm thời” effectively communicates temporary situations or decisions. It helps us express what is applicable at the moment but may change later.

examples.vi: “tạm thời”
examples.en: effectively communicates temporary situations or decisions. It helps us express what is applicable at the moment but may change later.

sections.heading: TẠM THỜI… = FOR NOW
sections.explanation: The expression “ tạm thời ” is often used as an adverb to indicate something that is valid or applicable only for the time being. PATTERN Tạm thời, Subject + Verb For

examples.vi: Tạm thời, tôi sẽ ở lại đây
stay here Tạm thời, chúng ta không cần lo lắng
to worry Tạm thời, cô ấy sẽ làm việc từ
work remotely Tạm thời, bạn nên giữ bí mật
examples.en: For now, you should keep it

examples.vi: a secret Tạm thời, tôi không muốn nói chuyện với bạn
examples.en: For now , I don’t want to talk

examples.vi: to you Tạm thời, cậu có thể sử dụng máy tính của tôi
examples.en: For now , you can use my computer

sections.heading: TẠM THỜI LÀ VẬY! = JUST FOR NOW!
sections.explanation: The phrase “tạm thời là vậy!” can be added at the end of a statement to emphasize acceptance of a temporary situation. It’s similar to saying “that’s just how it is for now!” in English. PATTERN Statement + tạm thời là vậy! Tôi sẽ ở lại đây. Tạm thời là vậy! For now, I will stay here Chúng ta không cần lo lắng. Tạm thời là vậy! For now , we don’t need to worry Cô ấy sẽ làm việc từ xa. Tạm thời là vậy! For now , she will work remotely Bạn nên giữ bí mật. Tạm thời là vậy! For now, you should keep it a secret Tôi không muốn nói chuyện với bạn. Tạm thời là vậy! For now , I don’t want to talk to you Cậu có thể sử dụng máy tính của tôi. Tạm thời là vậy! For now , you can use my computer
